rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA GLAVIN ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Avon Products, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Sealing Exhibits H-M To The Declaration Of Lisa Gallo Filed In Support Of Avons Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication; Declaration Of Elizabeth N. Branham
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2019, line 1. Defendant Avon Product, Inc.'s Motion for an order sealing Exhibits H-M to the declaration of Lisa Gallo filed in support of Avon's motion for summary judgment or, in the alterative, for summary adjudication is continued upon the Court's own motion to February 20, 2019 at 9:30 am in department 503. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of a reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
